name: Dyresteg (Roast Venison W/Goat Cheese Sauce)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 oz Brown Norwegian goat cheese (Gjet"st), finely diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 475°F.
2. Tie the roast tightly at 1/2 inch intervals with kitchen cord so it holds its shape while cooking.
3. Spread softened butter evenly over the meat using a pastry brush.
4. Place the roast on a rack in a shallow roasting pan and sear in the hot oven for about 20 minutes until browned.
5. Reduce heat to 375°F, sprinkle generously with salt and pepper.
6. Pour stock into the pan and cook, uncovered, for 1 1/4 hours, basting every half hour with pan juices using a large spoon or bulb baster.
7. Ensure internal temperature is slightly rare (150°F) on meat thermometer.
8. Remove roast to heated platter, cover loosely and rest in turned off oven while sauce is made.
9. Skim and discard fat from pan juices.
10. Measure remaining liquid; reduce to 1 cup by boiling or add water to reach 1 cup.
11. In a small, heavy saucepan, heat 1 tbsp butter and stir in 1 tbsp flour. Cook roux for 6-8 minutes over low heat until nut brown.
12. Avoid burning roux as it will impart bitter flavor.
13. Beat pan juices into the roux with wire whisk.
14. Whisk in jelly and cheese, beating to dissolve and achieve smooth sauce.
15. Stir in sour cream; do not allow sauce to boil.
16. Taste for seasoning, remove strings from roast, and carve meat into thin slices.
17. Serve sauce separately.
